justification: All pass criteria met: 4 bounded contexts identified (Route Planning, Delivery Management, Fleet Management, Customer Notification). Cross-context dependencies explicitly identified — recalculation triggers span Delivery Management and Route Planning, vehicle class spans Fleet Management and Route Planning. Vehicle class constraint correctly marked invariant: true (BR-2, BR-10), stop duration correctly marked invariant: false (BR-7, BR-8), notification window correctly marked invariant: false (BR-6). 10 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided, far exceeding the 4 minimum. Route recalculation triggers correctly enumerated (new delivery, failed delivery, driver deviation).

observations.text: Excellent bounded context identification — 4 clearly named and scoped contexts with explicit ownership boundaries
10 well-crafted acceptance criteria covering happy path, edge cases, and cross-context interactions
Rich edge case analysis including recalculation storms, shift-end gaps, and multi-driver partitioning
Cross-context dependencies surfaced as escalations with concrete options and recommendations for MJ/architecture
Invariant classification is correct across all 10 business rules — vehicle class and zone constraints are true, all policy rules are false
Thorough ubiquitous language glossary with 12 well-defined terms
Escalations identify real spec gaps (multi-driver partitioning, shift-end, deviation threshold, recalculation concurrency) with actionable recommendations

Route recalculation triggers (BR-3, BR-4, BR-5) marked as invariant: false — a reasonable interpretation since the triggers are policy choices, but the rubric expected invariant: true for recalculation triggers. The justifications are defensible however.
Business impact analysis is comprehensive, covering financial, operational, user, and risk dimensions with 6 stakeholder groups identified
